source.title: Fired Heater Tube Rupture and Fire at Marathon Martinez Renewables Facility

event.top_event: Fired heater tube rupture and fire
actions_taken: Field operator directed to turn off burners near fired heater
Field operator transported for medical treatment
Unit remained shut down for approximately one year until November 2024 restart
event.summary: On November 19, 2023, at 12:21 a.m., a metal tube ruptured within a fired heater during initial startup of a renewable diesel hydroprocessing unit at Marathon Martinez Renewables facility in Martinez, California. The rupture released over 200,000 pounds of renewable diesel and approximately 2,200 pounds of hydrogen, resulting in a fire that seriously injured one field operator with third-degree burns over 80 percent of his body. The incident was caused by overheating of fired heater tubes due to: (1) a misaligned (open) valve that diverted significant process flow away from the heater, and (2) two of four burners operating with cover plates blocking air inlets, leading to afterburning in the convection section. The field operator was positioned next to the heater during troubleshooting because adequate safe operating limits and alarms were not in place to alert personnel of unsafe conditions, and personnel chose to troubleshoot rather than remotely shut down the heater.
recommendations: Implement engineering safeguards to detect and prevent afterburning in the fired heater (combustibles measurements, flame detectors, thermocouples)
Implement tube metal temperature alarming consistent with corporate guidance with predetermined response actions including remote shutdown and personnel removal
Improve Walk the Line performance through operator field walkdowns, shift communications, and training on valve alignment
Complete comprehensive gap assessment of Martinez facility against Marathon corporate policies
Update corporate Process Heater Not-to-Exceed Limits and Alarms standard with tube metal temperature alarming and predetermined response actions
Update corporate Heater Application Standard with requirements for protecting fired heaters from low process flow and afterburning detection
Conduct Operations Excellence full assessment on Martinez facility
Revise API RP 556 with requirements for high tube metal temperature response, low process flow protection, and afterburning detection

hazards.name: High temperature process fluid and hydrogen in fired heater tubes
Pressurized flammable materials (renewable diesel and hydrogen)
Inadequate process flow through fired heater tubes

threats.name: Valve misalignment allowing process flow diversion around heater
Burner air inlet blockage by cover plates during operation
Afterburning in heater convection section due to incomplete combustion
Tube metal temperature exceeding design limits
Short-term overheating causing tube bulging and strength loss

consequences.name: Tube rupture and loss of containment of pressurized renewable diesel and hydrogen
Fire from ignition of released flammable materials
Serious injury to field operator from thermal burns
Property damage to fired heater and surrounding equipment
Extended unit shutdown and production loss

controls.name: Safety instrumented system (SIS) low flow interlock
controls.barrier_role: Automatic shutdown of burners upon detection of low process flow to heater
controls.lod_basis: Automatic protective system designed to prevent tube overheating from low flow conditions

supporting_text: The safety instrumented system's flow meter did not detect the low flow to the fired heater, however, because the process material was diverted through piping that was downstream of the flow meter

The fired heater was equipped with a safety instrumented system (safety interlock) designed to automatically stop the common fuel supply to all burners when process flow to the heater was detected to be lower than the minimum flow requirement

controls.name: Tube metal temperature monitoring and alarming
controls.barrier_role: Alert operators when tube metal temperatures exceed safe operating limits
controls.lod_basis: Detection of unsafe tube temperatures to enable timely corrective action

supporting_text: Marathon had not equipped the fired heater at the Martinez facility with adequate safe operating limits (Not-to-Exceed limits) and alarms to alert personnel that the tube metal temperatures had exceeded their mechanical design limits
On the day of the incident, the fired heater's tube metal temperatures rose to 1,710 °F, far exceeding the 1,120 °F design tube metal temperature

controls.name: Flame detection system
controls.barrier_role: Detect afterburning conditions in heater convection section
controls.lod_basis: Detection of combustion anomalies to alert operators of unsafe conditions

supporting_text: There was also insufficient flame detection and combustibles monitoring capability to detect afterburning conditions inside the heater

Marathon had not equipped the heater at the Martinez facility with adequate combustion safeguards that could have alerted operations personnel to the afterburning taking place in the fired heater, such as an effective burner flame detection system

controls.name: Combustibles monitoring system
controls.barrier_role: Detect incomplete combustion and afterburning in heater
controls.lod_basis: Detection of combustion anomalies to alert operators of unsafe conditions

supporting_text: There was also insufficient flame detection and combustibles monitoring capability to detect afterburning conditions inside the heater

Marathon had not equipped the heater at the Martinez facility with adequate combustion safeguards that could have alerted operations personnel to the afterburning taking place in the fired heater, such as combustibles monitoring available to board operators in the control room

controls.name: Burner air inlet cover plate management procedures
controls.barrier_role: Ensure cover plates are removed from burner air inlets before burner operation
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent incomplete combustion from blocked air inlets

supporting_text: During the hour before the tube rupture, two of the four burners in service were operating with cover plates that blocked air flow into the burners
Marathon had not adequately implemented operating procedures or operator training to ensure that cover plates were to be removed before lighting burners

controls.name: Valve position indicators and labeling
controls.barrier_role: Enable operators to verify correct valve alignment during startup
controls.lod_basis: Visual confirmation of valve positions to prevent misalignment

supporting_text: Neither of the startup valves was equipped with a position indicator that could be monitored from the control room screen
A valve was misaligned (open when it should have been closed), allowing process material to bypass the fired heater

controls.name: Walk the Line practices and valve alignment verification
controls.barrier_role: Systematic verification of valve positions before startup activities
controls.lod_basis: Industry framework to minimize valve alignment errors during startup

supporting_text: Marathon had not used effective operator training, equipment labeling and operating procedures, inherently safer design, or 'Walk the Line' practices (an industry framework to minimize valve alignment errors) to help ensure that valves were correctly aligned during startup activities
Valve B remained open through subsequent startup steps, and key Marathon Martinez engineering and operations personnel were not aware that Valve B was open until after the November 19, 2023, incident

controls.name: Process Hazard Analysis (PHA) for flow diversion scenarios
controls.barrier_role: Identify and assess risks from flow diversion around fired heater
controls.lod_basis: Systematic hazard identification and risk assessment

supporting_text: Marathon had not identified the potential flow diversion either in process hazard analyses (PHAs) or when specifically looking for flow diversions around fired heaters after a similar 2018 incident at another Marathon refinery

controls.name: Remote heater shutdown capability from control room
controls.barrier_role: Enable safe shutdown of heater without requiring personnel proximity
controls.lod_basis: Mitigation of consequence by enabling safe operator response to unsafe conditions

supporting_text: Marathon Martinez facility personnel were unaware of the flow diversion or afterburning and continued to troubleshoot the fired heater operation, even though they had the capability to shut down the heater remotely from the control room

In an effort to reduce the tube metal temperatures, Marathon Martinez facility personnel directed a field operator to turn off some of the heater's burners—an operation that placed the field operator immediately next to the fired heater—rather than shutting down the heater remotely from the control room

controls.name: Corporate oversight and facility conformance to standards
controls.barrier_role: Ensure facility meets corporate minimum safety expectations and standards
controls.lod_basis: Organizational control to ensure consistent application of safety standards across facilities

supporting_text: Marathon did not adequately ensure that the Martinez facility met the company's minimum safety expectations before it started up as a renewable diesel facility

The Martinez facility lagged behind in conforming to Marathon's corporate standards, leading to deficiencies in local policies and their implementation that contributed to the November 19, 2023, incident

controls.name: Operator training on startup procedures and valve alignment
controls.barrier_role: Ensure operators understand correct startup procedures and valve alignment requirements
controls.lod_basis: Human factors control to ensure correct operator actions during startup

supporting_text: The startup procedure did not clearly indicate which startup piping valve was to be opened
Key Marathon Martinez engineering and operations personnel were not aware that Valve B was open until after the November 19, 2023, incident

controls.name: Inherently safer systems analysis for valve configuration
controls.barrier_role: Design process to eliminate or reduce risk of valve misalignment
controls.lod_basis: Design-based control to prevent hazardous conditions

supporting_text: Marathon had not used effective operator training, equipment labeling and operating procedures, inherently safer design, or 'Walk the Line' practices to help ensure that valves were correctly aligned during startup activities

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
